content.heading: Loops — repeat code for every item

blocks.text: A for loop runs the same block of code repeatedly, counting from a start to an end. A forEach loop runs a function for each item in an array automatically — no counter variable needed.

blocks.desc: Start at 0, run while i is less than 5, add 1 each time
Run this function once for each item in the array

blocks.label: Which one to use
blocks.text: Use a for loop when you need the index number or want to count in a custom way. Use forEach when you just want to do something with each item.

exercises.title: Count with a loop
tasks: Write a for loop that logs every number from 1 to 10.
Write a for loop that logs only even numbers from 2 to 20.
Write a for loop that counts backwards from 10 to 1.
Challenge: write a loop that logs the square of each number from 1 to 5.
exercises.hint: For even numbers: i = i + 2 instead of i++. For backwards: start at 10, condition i > 0, use i--.

exercises.title: Loop over an array
tasks: Create an array of five numbers.
Use forEach to log each number multiplied by itself.
Use a for loop to add all numbers together into a total variable.
Log the total after the loop finishes.
exercises.hint: Declare total = 0 before the loop. Inside the loop: total = total + numbers[i].
